name: Bobby Rosengarden
abstract: Robert Marshall Rosengarden (April 23, 1924 – February 27, 2007) was an American jazz drummer, percussionist and bandleader. A native of Elgin, Illinois, United States, he played on many recordings and in television orchestras and talk show bands.

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: We had all different rhythms on this other one that I did... Bobby Rosengarden to play the 6-8 time, and me I'd just weave in between all of them.

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Igoe
about_quotes.evidence: Bobby Rosengarden helped me by hiring me at NBC, getting NBC to hire me as his sub
about_quotes.doc_title: Sonny Igoe interviewed by Monk Rowe, Emerson, New Jersey, July 31, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: we’ve got a concert in Hackensack with Bucky Pizzarelli, Milt Hinton, Bobby Rosengarden, Zoot Sims and Hank Jones and myself. Can you come? ... That was the beginning of an eight year association.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Mat Domber
about_quotes.evidence: So who did he pick? ... Bobby Rosengarden. I mean the people who were meaningful to him in his career... that got me started with a whole new group of people.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mat Domber interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: I remember a guy named Dennis — well first there was Bobby Rosengarden. When I first came up he was doing all the work.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Let's see, there's myself on drums, and Duke Hanna, and Mousey Alexander, Bobby Rosengarden - that's a motley crew!
